title: Adobe CJM ExperienceEvent - Custom Execution Metrics
description: Per-call execution telemetry for outbound HTTP message deliveries made through customer-configured channels. Present on delivery feedback events for custom channels (such as Viber and Kakao, and other customer-created channels). Absent for Email, SMS, Push, Web, In-App, Direct Mail, Inbox, and Message Feed deliveries, which use their own delivery infrastructure, and absent when a delivery is excluded before dispatch. The channel that produced the delivery is identified by the standard `messageProfile.channel._id` field on the same event — `channel._id` carries the per-channel identifier (for custom channels, the channel registry id) and `channel._type` carries the channel category.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Execution Type
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Identifies the kind of customer-configured HTTP execution this event represents. This is an open string (not constrained to a fixed list) so new execution kinds can be added without a schema change. The value `channel` denotes a custom-channel delivery; the channel itself is identified by `messageProfile.channel._id`.

meta:enum.channel: Outbound delivery through a customer-configured custom channel

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Endpoint
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: The configured endpoint URL in template form, with placeholders left un-substituted (for example, `https://api.viber.com/v1/{{profile.id}}/send`). The template form is stored rather than the per-recipient resolved URL to keep reporting cardinality manageable, and query parameters are removed. Absent when the delivery was skipped before any HTTP request was attempted.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: HTTP Method
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: HTTP method used for the call (for example, POST). Absent when the delivery was skipped.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Request Timestamp
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: UTC timestamp captured immediately before the HTTP request is sent. Absent when the delivery was skipped.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Response Time
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Time in milliseconds for the HTTP exchange with the customer endpoint, measured at the HTTP boundary only. It excludes authentication, connection setup, and any time spent waiting in the throttle queue (reported separately as `waitTime`). For a request that times out, this equals the configured timeout. Absent when the delivery was skipped.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Wait Time
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Time in milliseconds the request waited in the throttle queue before the HTTP exchange began. Not currently populated; reserved for future per-call throttle instrumentation.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Status Code
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: The HTTP response status code returned by the customer endpoint, preserved as the raw integer (for example, 401 or 503) for exact-code analysis. Absent when the delivery was skipped (no HTTP request) or timed out without a response.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Throttle Applicable
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Whether throttling is applicable to this delivery — that is, whether a throttling configuration is in effect at the time of dispatch. It does not indicate that this particular call actually waited in a queue.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Cache Applicable
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Whether response caching is applicable to this delivery — that is, whether a response-cache configuration is in effect at the time of dispatch. Absent for custom-channel deliveries, which do not use response caching; populated when a caching configuration applies.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Cache Hit
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: Whether this call was served from the response cache instead of reaching the customer endpoint. Meaningful only when `cacheApplicable` is true; absent for custom-channel deliveries.

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Error Code
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: A single error code describing the failure, covering both pre-dispatch failures (such as authentication errors) and HTTP-stage failures (4xx, 5xx, or timeout). Absent on success. This is an open string (not constrained to a fixed list) so the set of codes can evolve without a schema change.

meta:enum.AUTH_CACHE_IO: Failed to read or write the auth-token cache before the call
meta:enum.AUTH_HTTP_ERROR: The authentication request to obtain a token returned an HTTP error
meta:enum.AUTH_TOKEN_PARSE: The authentication token response could not be parsed
meta:enum.AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRED: The cached authentication token was expired and could not be refreshed
meta:enum.REQUEST_GENERATION_ERROR: The outbound request could not be constructed before dispatch
meta:enum.HTTP_4XX: The customer endpoint returned a 4xx client error
meta:enum.HTTP_5XX: The customer endpoint returned a 5xx server error
meta:enum.HTTP_TIMEOUT: The call exceeded the configured timeout
meta:enum.HTTP_PARSE_ERROR: The customer endpoint response could not be parsed

https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.title: Error Reason
https://ns.adobe.com/experience/customer.description: A human-readable description of the failure, such as an exception message or parsed error body. Intended for drilldown and debugging rather than aggregation; use the error code for grouping. Absent on success.
